items.title: Generic Hero and Card Grid
items.summary: A centered headline, two calls to action, and three equal feature cards used regardless of product or content needs.
items.intent: Flag template assembly that replaces product-specific structure and evidence with a familiar marketing sequence.
checks: Verify the first viewport reveals the actual offer, product, place, person, or user task.
warnings: Repeated equal cards often flatten hierarchy and force unrelated content into one shape.
exceptions: A genuinely equal set of three choices may be represented by three peer cards.

items.title: Card Everything
items.summary: Every section, metric, control group, and nested region receives its own rounded container and shadow.
items.intent: Restore hierarchy through layout, dividers, and whitespace instead of multiplying decorative frames.
checks: Remove containers that do not communicate an entity, selection, state, modal layer, or bounded tool.
warnings: Nested cards obscure ownership and reduce usable space.
exceptions: Repeated selectable objects or independent entities may need card boundaries.

items.title: Habitual Gradient and Glow
items.summary: Purple-blue gradients, blurred accents, glass, or glow are added by habit without brand, material, or state meaning.
items.intent: Make effects earn their presence through product context instead of serving as generic polish.
checks: For every effect, name the hierarchy, state, material, or brand purpose it serves.
warnings: Effects can reduce contrast, hide content, and make unrelated products look alike.
exceptions: An established brand system or expressive game may use these effects intentionally.

items.title: Oversized Tool Typography
items.summary: Hero-scale headings and generous marketing spacing are placed inside dense dashboards, panels, tables, or sidebars.
items.intent: Match display scale to the working context and preserve scan speed and information density.
checks: Confirm headings, labels, rows, and controls fit their container at supported widths and zoom.
warnings: Large type can push primary data and controls below the fold.
exceptions: A true empty state or onboarding moment may use larger display type within an application.

items.title: Universal Motion
items.summary: Every element fades upward, scales on hover, or receives `transition: all` regardless of interaction purpose.
items.intent: Replace decorative motion defaults with state feedback and spatial explanation that remain interruptible.
checks: Remove motion that does not communicate state, causality, hierarchy, or spatial relationship.
warnings: Universal motion creates distraction, layout cost, and reduced-motion gaps.
exceptions: A choreographed expressive sequence may animate many elements when it is the requested experience.

items.title: Fabricated Evidence
items.summary: Invented testimonials, customer logos, avatars, metrics, integrations, awards, or claims are presented as real.
items.intent: Protect user trust and prevent generated placeholders from becoming false product evidence.
checks: Use source-backed facts or label development examples and sample data clearly.
For a fictional concept, label the surface once and treat inventory, prices, policies, location, stock, ratings, and delivery promises as sample content.
warnings: Realistic-looking placeholder evidence is easily mistaken for a verified claim.
exceptions: Clearly labeled fictional data is acceptable for prototypes and tests.

items.title: Desktop Shrink
items.summary: Desktop columns, labels, toolbars, and interaction models are merely reduced in size for mobile.
items.intent: Require narrow-screen recomposition that preserves priority, target size, reading order, and state clarity.
checks: Verify grouping, order, disclosure, labels, and controls at a narrow mobile width.
warnings: Proportional shrinking produces clipped labels, tiny targets, and incoherent order.
exceptions: A fixed-format canvas may scale when zoom and pan are core, accessible interactions.

items.title: Mixed Design Systems
items.summary: Unrelated component libraries, icon families, token scales, or interaction conventions are combined in one surface.
items.intent: Maintain a coherent foundation and avoid duplicated dependencies, inconsistent states, and accessibility drift.
checks: Choose one primary component and token system, then justify any exception explicitly.
warnings: Visual restyling alone does not reconcile different keyboard and state behavior.
exceptions: Migration work may temporarily bridge systems behind a documented compatibility boundary.

items.title: Color-only State
items.summary: Selection, status, error, or chart meaning depends only on a hue change with no text, icon, shape, or position cue.
items.intent: Keep meaning available to users with color-vision differences and in low-contrast viewing conditions.
checks: Add a non-color cue and verify relevant text and non-text contrast.
warnings: A palette that looks distinct to the author may collapse under color-vision differences.
exceptions: Color may reinforce a state when another programmatic and visible cue already conveys it.

items.title: Atmospheric Subject Hiding
items.summary: Dark overlays, blur, aggressive crops, or stock-like media obscure the product, place, object, person, or state users need to inspect.
items.intent: Use media as evidence and usable content rather than as interchangeable atmosphere.
checks: Verify the primary subject is recognizable and materially inspectable in the first relevant viewport.
For commerce and object-focused pages, reject generic gradients, blobs, abstract geometry, or exploded decorative pieces as the primary product image.
warnings: Decorative overlays can hide detail and weaken trust.
exceptions: Atmospheric media may lead a narrative experience when detailed inspection is not the user goal.

items.title: Collapsed Spacing
items.summary: Content touches a visible border, peer items stick together, or responsive wrapping removes the separation needed to read grouping and hierarchy.
items.intent: Make breathing room an explicit layout relationship instead of an accidental result of margins, intrinsic width, or leftover container space.
checks: Inspect every bordered or filled surface for intentional content inset on each occupied edge.
Inspect vertical stacks, control clusters, wrapped rows, card grids, labels, and actions for explicit row and column gaps.
Verify focus rings, shadows, selected outlines, and validation messages do not touch or clip against neighboring content.
At mobile breakpoints, step down the spacing scale without collapsing ordinary relationships to zero.
warnings: A layout can avoid overlap and still feel broken when content is visually glued to its border or neighbor.
exceptions: Intentional seams are valid for segmented controls, tables, tiled media, continuous rules, and edge-to-edge crops.

items.title: Per-Section Posterization
items.summary: Every long-page section resets into another hero with giant display type, excessive lead-in space, outline words, numbered labels, or a full visual spectacle.
items.intent: Preserve one display crescendo and create section hierarchy through content, media, composition, density, and interaction instead of repeated headline scale.
checks: Use hero-scale typography at one display apex and at most one intentional secondary climax.
Step ordinary section headings down materially and remove empty bands that exist only to frame type.
Rewrite adjacent sections that repeat the same heading, spacing, and grid move.
warnings: Changing background color or alignment does not make repeated hero sections structurally different.
exceptions: An explicit poster, exhibition, or typographic narrative may use repeated display moments when the sequence itself is the concept and viewport fit remains intact.

items.title: Tiny Subject in a Decorative Stage
items.summary: The primary product or object appears at icon scale inside a large empty panel, often with multiple nested frames and surrounding labels doing more visual work than the subject.
items.intent: Make the subject carry the page through credible scale, crop, silhouette, and material detail instead of using decoration to compensate for a weak asset.
checks: Compare the subject bounding area with its media region at desktop and mobile sizes.
Remove nested frames that do not communicate product meaning.
Replace or regenerate icon-like primary art before polishing the surrounding stage.
warnings: A large media container does not create product authority when most of it is empty.
exceptions: A deliberately distant subject may be valid when scale and isolation are the narrative concept.

items.title: Visible Design Rationale
items.summary: Public copy explains the prompt, landing page, hero, layout, feature grid, generated output, or why the interface was composed a certain way.
items.intent: Keep internal generation reasoning out of the product experience and preserve a coherent brand voice.
checks: Rewrite implementation commentary as product meaning or remove it.
warnings: Meta copy makes a polished interface feel generated and breaks immersion.
exceptions: Documentation, case studies, and design-system education may explicitly discuss their own interface decisions.

items.title: House-Style Convergence
items.summary: Unrelated briefs repeatedly converge on the same macrostructure and motif bundle, such as dark canvas, fluorescent accent, mono section labels, hard grids, terminal chrome, and giant condensed type.
items.intent: Keep UI Forge product-derived by separating subject matter from style and rotating complete direction systems instead of recoloring a familiar shell.
checks: Name three tempting genre defaults before selection and use at most one without brand, asset, or reference evidence.
Compare against existing project surfaces or recent artifacts when available; reroute if the macrostructure and two or more strong motifs repeat.

Reject unsupported technical bundles such as condensed ultra-bold type, uppercase mono labels, fluorescent ticker, saturated artifact panel, offset shadow, and hard rules when three or more recur together.

Reject unsupported premium-commerce bundles such as cream canvas, brown or burgundy accents, display serif, floating pill navigation, soft rounded panels, and sepia material swatches when three or more recur together.
warnings: Each motif may be valid alone; the unsupported recurring combination is the failure.
exceptions: An established brand system or explicit reference may intentionally own the recurring combination.
